rulings.Case Title: MARKET STREET SF HOTEL HOLDINGS, LLC VS. 1095 MARKET STREET OWNER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Receiver For An Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2023 line 1. OTHER JEFFREY KOLESSAR (RECEIVER) Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Receiver For An Order 1) Approving The Receiver Final Report And Final Accounting And Approvig The Fees And Costs And Discharging The Receiver Hearing Required to address the timeliness of the filing of the motion. While the motion was served in compliance with CCP 1005 it was not filed "at least 16 court days before the hearing." CCP 1005. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HENRY LUCERO ET AL VS. JASON ESTAVILLO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT JASON ESTAVILLO, DON ODELL, ELIZABETH CERMAK, LAW OFFICE JASON W. ESTAVILLO PC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The Notice of demurrer does not comply with CRC 3.1110(a) - no grounds for the motion stated and the nature of the motion appears to be identified as demurrer "to complaint," while the demurrer and the Points and Authorities only provide grounds and arguments as to two of the three causes of action, not the entire complaint. Furthermore, moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and breach of contract fail to state a claim per CCP 430.10(e) due to being duplicative of the cause of action for professional negligence. The court notes that additional arguments were raised in the reply and those arguments are not considered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ZHIYU JIANG VS. IRINA RIBEIRO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT IRINA RIBEIRO MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. Motion was e-served and filed on March 13, 2023 for April 6, 2023 hearing. There are only 17 court days between these dates. Additionally, this motion appears to be moot. The writ of possession was retuned "satisfied" on March 22, 2023.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: J&N INTERNATIONAL ENTERPRISES, LLC VS. ALICIA FLORES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT This motion will be heard at 10:00 a.m. on April 6, 2023 in Department 503 by the honorable Samuel Feng per the order of the presiding judge. Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Moving party shifted their burden and is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. The operative notice is fatally defective as it fails to comply with CCP 1161(2). Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a landlord may limit the payment of rent to only one method of delivery, in-person, and disallow any payment by mail. An election to accept payment in person is within the landlord's discretion, however elimination of the option to pay by mail per CCP 1161(2) is not. "Under the clear language of the statute, the decision to allow personal payment of the rent, in addition to allowing payment by mail by the tenant, is up to the landlord." Hsieh v. Pederson (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th Supp. 1. =(503/SKF)
